Healthy eating tips
Easy as tips to eat healthier
Learn more →
🧂
Salt's sneaking in aye.
Most of us are smashing nearly double the daily limit without even touching the shaker. Keep an eye on that sodium pill.
Heart Foundation NZ
💪
Protein's your best mate.
Get 20g+ in your kai and you'll actually feel full after. No more sad desk hunger at 3pm aye.
NZ Nutrition Foundation
🫀
Sat fat's the sneaky one — not all fat.
24g is your daily limit. Anything fried, creamy or cheesy is gonna stack it up quick.
Health Navigator NZ
⚡
Kjs add up mean.
One feed can chew through half your daily 8,700kJ just like that. Worth a cheeky check before you order aye.
FSANZ
🥦
Fibre — the unsung hero.
Keeps you full and your guts happy. Most of us aren't getting enough. Wraps, pitas and sides with a bit of mahi in them are your best bet.
Heart Foundation NZ

Small shifts,
big results.
Bite-sized, evidence-backed tips on eating better in Aotearoa — new picks every day.

bar_chart
NZ Fast Food Reality
swipe1 / 5
What the data says about fast food in Aotearoa
OBESITY
schedule20 SEC
1 in 3
NZ adults classified as obese
1 in 3 NZ adults are obese
Poor diet is the leading driver of obesity in NZ — one of the highest rates in the OECD.
Health New Zealand — Te Whatu Ora, 2023
swipe_leftSwipe to keep learning
FAST FOOD
schedule25 SEC
40%+
of daily energy from ultra-processed foods
Ultra-processed food is rising
Ultra-processed foods make up over 40% of energy intake for many NZ adults.
NZ Nutrition Foundation, 2022
DIABETES
schedule20 SEC
250k+
New Zealanders with type 2 diabetes
Type 2 diabetes is surging
Over 250,000 New Zealanders have type 2 diabetes — and diet is a key driver.
Diabetes New Zealand, 2023
HEART DISEASE
schedule20 SEC
90 min
between each NZ heart-disease death
Leading cause of death in NZ
Cardiovascular disease kills one New Zealander every 90 minutes.
Heart Foundation NZ, 2023
TAMARIKI
schedule25 SEC
1 in 5
NZ tamariki classified as obese
1 in 5 NZ children are obese
NZ childhood obesity is among the worst in the developed world — habits formed young last a lifetime.
Health New Zealand — Te Whatu Ora, 2023
restaurant
Healthy Portions
swipe1 / 5
Simple portion habits that make a real difference
PLATE RULE
schedule20 SEC
½ + ¼ + ¼
veg · protein · wholegrains
Half your plate = veggies
Half veggies, quarter protein, quarter wholegrains — simple and evidence-backed.
Health New Zealand — Te Whatu Ora
PORTION SIZE
schedule20 SEC
1 fist
= one standard serve of carbs
Your fist = one serve of carbs
Fast food portions are often 2–3× a standard serve — your fist is a good guide.
NZ Nutrition Foundation
EAT SLOWER
schedule20 SEC
20 min
for your brain to feel full
It takes 20 mins to feel full
Your brain takes 20 minutes to register fullness — eating slowly helps you stop in time.
Health New Zealand — Te Whatu Ora
UPSIZING
schedule20 SEC
+800 kJ
for upsizing — share instead
Share a side instead of going large
Upsizing adds 500–800kJ for a few cents — one of the easiest calories to skip.
NZ Nutrition Foundation
ORDER SMART
schedule20 SEC
~60%
of a main = a typical entrée
Order the entrée, not the main
Fast food mains are often 1.5–2× an entrée — starting smaller usually does the job.
Heart Foundation NZ
fitness_center
Protein & Your Body
swipe1 / 5
Why protein is your best friend for health and weight
SATIETY
schedule20 SEC
Fuller, longer
Protein lowers hunger hormones
Protein keeps you fuller, longer
Protein reduces hunger hormones — high-protein meals mean fewer cravings all day.
NZ Nutrition Foundation
METABOLISM
schedule25 SEC
20–30%
burned just digesting protein
Protein burns more calories to digest
Your body burns 20–30% of protein calories just digesting it — more than any other macro.
NZ Nutrition Foundation
DAILY TARGET
schedule25 SEC
0.8–1 g/kg
of body weight each day
Aim for 0.8–1g per kg bodyweight
Aim for 0.8–1g per kg of bodyweight daily — more if you're active.
Ministry of Health NZ
FAST FOOD PICKS
schedule30 SEC
Pita · Sushi · Grilled
best fast food protein picks
Best fast food protein sources in NZ
Grilled chicken, pita wraps and sushi rolls are your best fast food protein picks.
KaiCheck nutritional data
MUSCLE
schedule20 SEC
Keep muscle
Lose fat, not tone
Protein preserves muscle when losing weight
Enough protein while dieting means you lose fat — not muscle.
Sport New Zealand
water_drop
Sugar & Diabetes
swipe1 / 5
What sugar does to your body over time
SUGAR LIMIT
schedule20 SEC
< 50 g
daily added-sugar limit
NZ recommends under 50g sugar/day
A single large soft drink can hit the entire daily sugar limit in one go.
Ministry of Health NZ
BLOOD SUGAR
schedule20 SEC
Spike · Crash
more cravings in about an hour
Spikes and crashes fuel cravings
Sugar spikes cause crashes that trigger hunger again within an hour — protein slows this.
Diabetes New Zealand
TYPE 2 RISK
schedule25 SEC
Insulin resistance
precursor to type 2 diabetes
Consistent high sugar = insulin resistance
Consistently high sugar intake leads to insulin resistance — a precursor to type 2 diabetes.
Diabetes New Zealand
DRINKS
schedule20 SEC
Liquid sugar
the sneakiest source — swap for water
Liquid sugar is the sneakiest source
Swapping sugary drinks for water is one of the single highest-impact changes you can make.
Health New Zealand — Te Whatu Ora
SMART SWAPS
schedule25 SEC
Low GI
wholegrains, legumes, protein
Choose low-GI options where possible
Low-GI foods release energy slowly — wholegrains, legumes and proteins are all good choices.
Diabetes New Zealand
favorite
Saturated Fat & Heart
swipe1 / 5
How saturated fat affects your heart over time
DAILY LIMIT
schedule20 SEC
20–24 g
daily sat. fat limit for adults
Stay under 20–24g sat. fat per day
One fast food meal can easily hit the entire daily saturated fat limit of 20–24g.
Heart Foundation NZ
CHOLESTEROL
schedule25 SEC
LDL ↑
"bad" cholesterol builds in arteries
Sat. fat raises LDL cholesterol
Saturated fat raises LDL — the "bad" cholesterol that builds up in artery walls.
Heart Foundation NZ
COOKING
schedule20 SEC
-60%
sat. fat when grilled vs fried
Grilled beats fried — every time
Grilled chicken has up to 60% less saturated fat than the fried equivalent.
Heart Foundation NZ
HIDDEN SOURCES
schedule20 SEC
+4–6 g
sat. fat from extras alone
Cheese and sauces add up fast
Extra cheese or mayo can add 4–6g of saturated fat before you've even noticed.
Ministry of Health NZ
GOOD FATS
schedule25 SEC
Unsat. fats
fish · avocado · nuts
Swap saturated for unsaturated fat
Swapping sat. fat for unsaturated fats from fish, avocado and nuts is evidence-backed.
Heart Foundation NZ
directions_run
Move More, Feel Better
swipe1 / 5
Exercise benefits that go well beyond burning calories
NZ GUIDELINES
schedule20 SEC
150 min/wk
≈ 20 minutes a day
150 mins of movement per week
Just 20 minutes of movement a day meets the NZ guideline — and burns 400–500kJ.
Sport New Zealand
MENTAL HEALTH
schedule25 SEC
10 min walk
measurably lifts mood
Exercise reduces anxiety and depression
Exercise is as effective as medication for mild depression — even a 10-minute walk helps.
Sport New Zealand
MUSCLE
schedule20 SEC
2–3×/week
resistance preserves muscle
Muscle burns more energy at rest
Muscle burns calories even while you sleep — resistance training 2–3x a week preserves it.
Sport New Zealand
CHRONIC DISEASE
schedule25 SEC
-58% · -35%
type 2 · heart disease risk
Exercise cuts your disease risk significantly
Regular exercise cuts type 2 diabetes risk by up to 58% and heart disease by 35%.
Ministry of Health NZ
SLEEP
schedule20 SEC
Deeper sleep
fewer cravings next day
Better exercise = better sleep
Better exercise leads to better sleep — which reduces hunger hormones and makes healthy eating easier.
Sport New Zealand
auto_awesome

Pop back tomorrow for fresh tips, or head home and put today's learnings to work.
